commit.message:
improve: upgrade AgentPropsMatchup with 4 new adjustment factors + fix team stats

Bug fix:
- run_props_fetch now calls fetch_and_store_team_stats() so pace/defensive
  ratings are populated (previously fell back to league defaults for every team)

New adjustment factors (all multiplicative, individually capped):
1. Home/Away PPG split (±12% cap): uses player_game_log.is_home to compute
   location-specific avg; applied when n≥5 games at that location
2. H2H vs opponent (±20% cap): player's historical avg vs this specific team
   from player_game_log.opponent_abbr; applied when n≥3 matchups
3. Rest days extended: B2B ×0.92 (existing), 3+ days rest ×1.03 (new boost);
   rest_days field added to PlayerMatchupReport
4. Scoring trend (±5% cap): (l5/l10 - 1) × 0.30 transferred to projection;
   detects hot/cold streaks without overweighting short samples

New log line shows all factors:
  base=X pace=X def=X ha=X h2h=X rest=X trend=X → proj=X σ=X

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: settings cog custom speed menu 0.25x-8x with keyboard nav and theme detection (v10)

Intercepts click on YouTube's "Playback speed" menu item in the settings
cog and replaces the native submenu with a custom panel offering speeds
from 0.25x to 8x in 0.25 increments. YouTube's built-in menu only goes
up to 2x.

Implementation:
- MutationObserver watches for settings menu to appear, attaches click
  interceptor to the speed menu item
- Custom panel anchored to #movie_player with dark/light theming based
  on ytp-dark class detection (no runtime getComputedStyle)
- Back button in header returns to YouTube's top-level settings menu
- Keyboard nav: arrow keys to browse, Enter to select, Escape to go back
- Outside-click dismissal with capture-phase listener
- Graceful degradation: if YouTube changes selectors, the interceptor
  silently fails and native speed menu works as usual
- Settings cog integration only runs on desktop (skipped on m.youtube.com)
- MAX_SPEED raised from 4.0 to 8.0 (applies to keyboard shortcuts too)

Co-authored-by: npezarro <npezarro@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix NaN: numerical stability in model + better NaN handling in training loop

Three root causes of NaN identified and fixed:

1. Hopfield temperature (hopfield_memory.py): log_temp.exp() could underflow
   to near-zero, causing division by ~0 → Inf scores → NaN. Fixed by clamping
   log_temp to [-4, 4] range (temp between 0.018 and 54.6).

2. Unbounded phase (oscillatory_block.py): phase values grew without bound,
   causing cos/sin to lose all precision in bfloat16. Fixed by wrapping phase
   to [-pi, pi] after each update.

3. Magnitude overflow (oscillatory_block.py): large mag_ffn values overflowed
   in F.softplus with bfloat16. Fixed by clamping to [-20, 20] before softplus.

Training loop fix: NaN is now checked BEFORE backward() to prevent gradient
corruption, and the step counter advances even on all-NaN steps so training
doesn't get stuck in an infinite loop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add opt-in namespace dependency resolver

NewNamespaceResolver() creates edges from every namespaced object to
its Namespace. Opt-in via WithNamespaceDeps() — not in NewDefault()
because it adds an edge per namespaced object.

Bidirectional: forward checks Namespace existence, reverse uses
ListByNamespace index when a Namespace is added. Extract emits
unconditionally for dangling-reference detection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Relax trajectory optimizer limits for velocity compensation

The compensated aim target is slightly farther than the hub, requiring
more energy. The previous limits were too tight and caused the optimizer
to reject valid compensated shots:

- Min clearance: 2.0 -> 1.5 inches (40° descent at 1.8in was failing)
- Max peak height: 10 -> 16 feet (no physical ceiling on FRC field)
- Max RPM: 4000 -> 5000 (compensated shots need slightly more velocity)

All values remain tunable on the dashboard.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

the GTD25 logo when the sidebar is hidden, so mobile users can

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix velocity compensation dropping out during shoot-on-move

Two fixes to prevent the trajectory from suddenly losing compensation:

1. Remove the 1.5m aim offset clamp — the smart launch speed gates
   already prevent shooting at speeds where the offset would be
   unreasonable, and the clamp was causing the trajectory visualization
   to diverge from the actual ball path.

2. Keep last successful compensated aim target when refinement iteration
   fails, instead of falling back to the raw hub target. This prevents
   the compensation from dropping to zero when the optimizer can't
   achieve the compensated shot at certain positions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Phase 3 — environment profiling, coverage KB, and exposure weighting engine

Add environment profiling system that transforms the optimizer from a
generic visualization tool into a personalized security posture analyzer.
Users describe their infrastructure and security tooling, and the app
automatically adjusts per-technique exposure scores using real-world
product coverage data.

- Coverage Knowledge Base: 25 enterprise security tools (EDR, SIEM,
  network, email, hardening, vuln mgmt, identity) with 391 technique
  coverage entries sourced from MITRE Evals ER6/ER7, vendor Navigator
  layers, and Sigma rules. 15 infrastructure items across identity,
  cloud, OS, email, and virtualization categories. ICS KB with 3 OT
  monitoring tools and 3 PLC vendor exposure profiles.

- Exposure Weighting Engine: 3-step algorithm computing per-technique
  scores — base exposure from infrastructure, complementary product
  coverage reduction from security tools (defense-in-depth model),
  optional threat actor overlay multiplier (1.0–1.5x).

- Profile Wizard: 5-step modal (org context, infrastructure, security
  tools, threat actors, review). Searchable multi-select with category
  grouping. Progressive disclosure with skip on optional steps.
  Persisted to localStorage.

- Exposure Summary Panel: 4-metric dashboard (avg exposure, avg
  coverage, high-exposed count, well-covered count), top 5 uncovered
  chokepoints ranked by exposure × betweenness, CSV export.

- Visual integration: purple dotted ring on profile-covered graph nodes,
  coverage source detail in technique panel, enhanced Navigator export
  with coverage source comments.

Add ABC fuzz test suite — 22/22 passing, 256 runs each

Property-based tests covering all bonding curve mechanisms:
- Conservation invariant preserved after bond, burn, and allocate
- Price monotonicity (bond increases, burn decreases spot price)
- Tribute accounting (entry/exit tributes route to funding pool)
- No free tokens on roundtrip (arbitrage resistance)
- Quote-execution consistency for both bond and burn
- Multi-user interaction properties (later buyer gets fewer tokens)
- Supply-reserve consistency across all operations

docs: log round 16 - parse_group_response robustness

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: restore native XDP on loss cluster + per-interface XDP mode

The GRE tunnel interface (gr-0-0-0) doesn't support native XDP, and the
all-or-nothing fallback logic was forcing ALL interfaces into generic
XDP mode — including the Mellanox ConnectX-5 25G interfaces that support
native XDP perfectly. This caused a 60% throughput regression (22.5 →
8 Gbps) on the loss cluster.

Two fixes:
1. Skip tunnel interfaces in the native XDP probe loop — they always
   use generic mode but should not force the entire system to generic.
2. Add IFACE_FLAG_NATIVE_XDP to iface_zone_map so BPF can detect XDP
   mode per-interface and skip unnecessary CHECKSUM_PARTIAL detection
   on native XDP interfaces (saves ~10-30 insns per packet).

This preserves the CHECKSUM_PARTIAL fix for generic XDP (virtio local
cluster) while restoring the native XDP performance optimization on
hardware that supports it.

Verified: loss cluster IPv4 22.5 Gbps, IPv6 19.0 Gbps (baseline).
Local cluster TCP still works correctly with generic XDP.
